sourceTitle: Unleashing collaboration with Notion's Jira integration

questions.question: Who must initially set up the Jira Sync integration in Notion?

options.text: Any workspace member
options.explanation: Only workspace members can create syncs after the initial setup, but they cannot perform the first connection.

options.text: A workspace owner who is also a Jira admin
options.explanation: The guide states a workspace owner (who must also be a Jira admin) creates the first API token and connects Notion to Jira.

options.text: The Notion support team
options.explanation: Notion support does not perform the integration; it is done by the user's admin.

options.text: Any Jira project lead
options.explanation: Project leads may not have the required Notion permissions; a workspace owner is required.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of using a "linked view" instead of creating a new sync for the same Jira project?

options.text: It creates a copy of the data for offline use
options.explanation: Linked views do not duplicate data; they reference the existing sync.

options.text: It prevents duplicate data and keeps updates in sync
options.explanation: The guide recommends linked views to avoid data proliferation and ensure smoother updates.

options.text: It automatically grants edit permissions to all members
options.explanation: Permissions are still controlled by the original sync's settings.

options.text: It disables real‑time updates from Jira
options.explanation: Linked views continue to receive real‑time updates from the original sync.

questions.question: When consolidating Jira issues with the My Tasks database, how can you visually distinguish Jira‑synced items?

options.text: They appear in a separate section at the bottom
options.explanation: The guide mentions a visual icon, not a separate section.

options.text: A Jira icon appears next to the task
options.explanation: Jira‑synced issues are marked with a Jira icon for quick identification.

options.text: They are highlighted in red text
options.explanation: Color coding is not described; the icon is the cue.

options.text: They are listed under a different database name
options.explanation: They reside in the same My Tasks database, not a separate one.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as a best practice for connecting Jira and Notion?

options.text: Set up permissions for Jira sync
options.explanation: Managing permissions is explicitly recommended.

options.text: Authenticate your account when adding or editing projects
options.explanation: Authentication is part of the best‑practice list.

options.text: Create a new sync for every teamspace, even if data already exists
options.explanation: The guide advises using linked views instead of duplicate syncs.

options.text: Customize database views for different team needs
options.explanation: Custom views are encouraged as a best practice.

questions.question: In the recommended stand‑up page setup, which Notion block type is used to embed a live view of Jira sprint data?

options.text: Embedded video block
options.explanation: A video block would not display Jira data.

options.text: Linked view of database block
options.explanation: The guide tells you to use a linked view of the Jira sprint database.

options.text: Code snippet block
options.explanation: Code snippets are unrelated to displaying sprint information.

options.text: Table block created manually
options.explanation: While you could create a table, the live sync requires a linked view.

questions.question: How can you automate the creation of a new Notion page when a new epic is added in Jira?

options.text: Enable the "Create new pages" toggle in the Jira sync settings
options.explanation: There is no such toggle; automation is set up via Notion's integration features.

options.text: Set up a Notion automation that uses the Jira epic as a trigger to populate a PRD template
options.explanation: The guide describes this exact automation scenario.

options.text: Manually copy the epic details into a Notion page each time
options.explanation: Automation eliminates the need for manual copying.

options.text: Create a synced block that mirrors the Jira epic’s description
options.explanation: Synced blocks don’t create new pages; they only display content.

questions.question: What purpose do rollups serve in the context of Jira‑Notion integration?

options.text: They duplicate Jira issues into multiple Notion databases
options.explanation: Rollups aggregate data; they don’t duplicate issues.

options.text: They calculate summary metrics, such as completion rates, across linked records
options.explanation: The guide mentions rollups for showing percentage of completed tasks per sprint.

options.text: They create visual timelines of Jira sprints
options.explanation: Timelines are a different view type; rollups summarize data.

options.text: They automatically assign Jira tickets to team members
options.explanation: Assignment is handled in Jira, not via Notion rollups.

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the role of the Admin API token in Jira Sync?

options.text: It grants read‑only access to Jira projects for all workspace members
options.explanation: The token is used by the integration itself, not to give read‑only rights to members.

options.text: It allows the Notion integration to sync data on behalf of the workspace without relying on individual user tokens
options.explanation: The guide specifies that the Admin API token enables a unified experience across the workspace.

options.text: It replaces the need for any user authentication when adding new Jira projects
options.explanation: Personal authentication is still required for private projects and to identify the user.

options.text: It is only needed for connecting to Jira Server, not Jira Cloud
options.explanation: The integration works with all Jira Cloud products; the token is not limited to Server.

questions.question: When customizing the property display of a synced Jira issue database, what is the recommended way to hide unnecessary fields?

options.text: Delete the unwanted properties from the Notion database
options.explanation: Deleting would remove data from the sync; you should hide instead.

options.text: Click the three‑dot icon next to the synced view and toggle property visibility
options.explanation: The guide shows using the three‑dot menu to customize which properties are shown.

options.text: Create a separate filtered view that excludes those columns
options.explanation: While possible, the guide specifically mentions the three‑dot menu for property display.

options.text: Adjust the Jira project’s field configuration to remove the fields
options.explanation: Changing Jira config is unnecessary; Notion offers UI controls for hiding fields.

questions.question: Which automation example demonstrates how Notion can push updates back to a communication channel when a Jira issue changes status?

options.text: Sending a Slack message to a channel when a task is marked "Done" in Jira and synced to Notion
options.explanation: The guide describes this exact automation: a Slack notification triggered by a Jira status change.

options.text: Posting a comment on the Jira ticket when a Notion page is edited
options.explanation: The guide does not mention bidirectional commenting.

options.text: Creating a new Confluence page when a Jira epic is archived
options.explanation: Confluence is not part of the described Notion integration.

options.text: Automatically assigning a Jira issue to a team member when a Notion task is completed
options.explanation: Assignment automation is not covered; the example focuses on Slack notifications.
